name: Saccharomyces cerevisiae (BY4741)
shortDescription: GRAS baker's yeast — the model eukaryote. Being a eukaryote, it can host human nuclear receptors and GPCRs, unlocking sensing of steroids, hormones, and other eukaryotic-specific ligands that bacteria cannot detect. dCas9 CRISPRi/CRISPRa are well established.

safety.grasBasis: FDA GRAS; millennia of safe use in baking and brewing; EFSA QPS.
safety.biocontainment: Auxotrophic markers (his3, leu2, ura3, met15) standard; well-developed containment.
safety.notes: Eukaryote (fungus), not a bacterium — enables nuclear-receptor and GPCR-based biosensors.

engineering.crisprTools: dCas9 CRISPRi/CRISPRa established in yeast (Gilbert/Smith labs).
